title: antrophyum immersum
intro: Antrophyum immersum, commonly known as the water fern or submersed fern, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Antrophyum immersum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
